contract_traceability: research/02_lifecycle_contract.yaml — novelty claims, baseline requirements, evaluation requirements, blocking unknowns
architect/04_design_justification.md — traceability table mapping 15 contract items to architecture decisions
coder/model.py — ArthritisMILModel implements all 7 pipeline stages
coder/config.py — ModelConfig with all hyperparameters including train/eval toggles
validator/test_model.py — comprehensive test suite for all layers

baseline_status.image_level_vit: NOT_IMPLEMENTED — image-level ViT baseline module not built; ablation config exists
baseline_status.per_joint_cnn_yolo_resnet: PARTIAL — detection module exists; per-joint ResNet classifier not separated from MIL aggregator
baseline_status.single_disease_specialists: NOT_IMPLEMENTED — one-vs-all ablation config exists (ova_ra, ova_psa, ova_oa) but not trained/evaluated
baseline_status.frozen_vs_full_ft_ablation: IMPLEMENTED — backbone_frozen toggle in ablations; full_ft ablation config ready
baseline_status.detection_vs_tile_ablation: PARTIAL — detection_model='none' with tile config exists; tile-sampling module not implemented

grounded: RA scoring SOTA demonstrates feasibility of automated X-ray assessment for arthritis
No existing system jointly discriminates RA/PsA/OA at per-joint granularity
MIL frameworks for RA-only scoring exist and are validated (Bo et al. 2025)
Foundation models for MSK X-rays exist but are unvalidated for inflammatory arthritis
Explanation loss approaches (Uddin et al. 2025) are applicable but not yet applied to arthritis
hypothesis: MIL for three-way arthritis discrimination outperforms image-level models and single-disease specialists
Foundation model backbones provide meaningful label efficiency gains for this task
Per-joint attention weights correlate with disease-specific anatomical priors
Multi-view fusion improves over single-view models for arthritis classification
TODO_unverified: Minimum dataset size required for stable three-way training
Whether joint-level annotations are bounding-box or label-level
Whether disease-specific anatomical priors are available as structured data
Whether OrthoFoundation/SKELEX weights are publicly accessible
Inter-annotator agreement on per-joint disease labeling
Whether 6 months is sufficient for both model development AND clinical validation/interpretability analysis

evidence: Three-way RA-vs-PsA-vs-OA per-joint discrimination is a verified gap (literature review in research/01_landscape_synthesis.md)
Anatomy-guided explanation loss is novel for arthritis XAI (extends Uddin et al. 2025)
Detection-based MIL with foundation model backbone is a novel combination for rheumatology X-ray
concerns: Novelty depends on real dataset performance — synthetic benchmarks cannot validate clinical novelty
Architecture novelty is incremental (combines known components); the novelty is in the problem formulation and combination
Foundation model adaptation (Gap D) is a standard transfer learning baseline, not a novel method

evidence: All 7 architect-suggested ablations are defined and runnable (run_ablations.py)
pytest suite covers 30+ tests across shapes, gradients, numerics, CV properties, and loss correctness
Profiling script for memory/FLOPs estimation (profile_model.py)
Multi-view fusion tested in both concatenative and cross-attention modes
concerns: Baseline models (image-level ViT, single-disease specialists) are specified but not implemented as runnable modules
Evaluation requires real X-ray data — synthetic data provides only mechanical correctness checks
Calibration analysis (ECE) not yet implemented
Deletion/Insertion faithfulness metrics not yet implemented
No real-data benchmark that can distinguish the proposed architecture from trivial baselines

evidence: 10 inductive biases documented with one-sentence justifications
15 contract-to-architecture traceability mappings in design justification
5 implementation risk flags with mitigation strategies and falsification conditions
Training dynamics documented (order of convergence, expected loss curves)
Compute budget estimated per component (detection, backbone, MIL, heads)
concerns: Some inductive biases are asserted rather than empirically tested (e.g., 'per-image normalization preserves anatomical contrast')
The permutation invariance of MIL is noted as a feature but also loses spatial structure information — this trade-off is under-discussed

evidence: Smoke test provides shape and gradient flow verification
Ablation runner produces metrics on synthetic data
concerns: No results on real clinical data — cannot assess if the architecture actually works for arthritis
No error analysis (confusion matrices, failure cases, per-disease breakdown)
No comparison to literature SOTA numbers (requires actual training)
Synthetic data results have no scientific meaning for the clinical task

evidence: All hyperparameters in single ModelConfig dataclass with __post_init__ validation
Seed fixed at 42
All modules have clear shape contracts in docstrings
Smoke test runs end-to-end and validates all output shapes
Gradient checkpointing implemented for memory-constrained training
concerns: Deterministic training not enforced (torch.use_deterministic_algorithms)
Data preprocessing pipeline not included (requires real data)
YOLOv7 dependency not pinned to specific version/commit

evidence: Architecture diagram in ASCII (03_architecture_diagram.md)
Comprehensive design justification with traceability (04_design_justification.md)
Research landscape synthesis with gap analysis (01_landscape_synthesis.md)
Implementation roadmap with monthly milestones (03_implementation_roadmap.md)
Inductive bias table accessible to non-experts
concerns: Figures are ASCII art — thesis-ready figures would need professional rendering
Some technical depth is lost in the explanation of why DINOv2 transfers to X-ray (asserted rather than empirically shown)
No abstract or executive summary that states the full contribution in non-technical terms

blocking_gaps.description: No baseline comparison results exist — architecture has not been shown to beat image-level ViT or per-joint CNN baselines
Calibration (ECE), explanation faithfulness (Deletion/Insertion), and stratified evaluation not implemented
Validation on real clinical X-ray data is required to assess clinical utility; synthetic-only validation insufficient
Image-level ViT baseline and single-disease specialist models not built as runnable modules
'Foundation model provides label efficiency gains' — no experiment run to verify this claim
Three-way discrimination claim depends on real data performance; synthetic benchmarks cannot falsify or confirm
Tile-sampling MIL (ablation 7) is only partially implemented — no actual grid-based tile sampler
Full evaluation suite requires real X-ray dataset not included in repository

recommended_next_experiments.description: Train image-level ViT, per-joint CNN, and proposed MIL model on actual X-ray data. Compare per-joint macro-F1, patient-level AUROC.
recommended_next_experiments.priority: P0 — must complete for thesis results chapter

recommended_next_experiments.description: Compute explanation Dice between attention weights and RA/PsA/OA joint priors on real data. Compare with and without anatomy prior loss.
recommended_next_experiments.priority: P0 — must complete for XAI results

recommended_next_experiments.description: Compare frozen DINOv2, LoRA fine-tuned, and full fine-tuning on held-out test set. Report per-disease F1.
recommended_next_experiments.priority: P1 — validates foundation model adaptation claim

recommended_next_experiments.description: Compute ECE per disease class. Apply temperature scaling if ECE > 0.10. Report calibration curves.
Compute Deletion and Insertion AUC metrics on held-out test set. Compare attention-based and Grad-CAM explanations.
Stratify performance by disease severity (mild/moderate/severe), joint site (MCP/PIP/DIP/wrist), and image view (PA/oblique).
Compare three-way (RA/PsA/OA/normal) vs. one-vs-all binary classifiers. Measure multi-class penalty.
Evaluate how YOLOv7 detection thresholds affect downstream classification accuracy. Test confidence thresholds 0.3–0.9.
